body: Hi - I'd like to pick up 2 quart jars of wildflower honey this week. What times work, and is it still $20 a quart? Can come by after 5 most days.
Yeah, $20 a quart. Got both jars set aside. Pickup after 5 works. I'm off site by then. Text when you're 10 min out and I'll have them at the door.
Grabbed them last night. Thanks, both perfect.
Is the spring wildflower in stock yet? Want more like the pint I got at the market. If not ready, when's the next batch?
Spring batch isn't jarred yet. Next harvest goes in jars late spring, ready early summer. Got fall wildflower now if you want some in the meantime. $12 a pint.
Fall works. Put me down for two pints, I'll swing by Saturday.
Grocery desk at Ridgemont Co-op. Low on the 1 lb jars. Can you do a case of 12 and send wholesale pricing? Want it shelved before the weekend.
Do you have comb honey right now? My mother used to buy it, want to get her a couple pieces. Pay whatever it runs.
Got a few comb pieces left. $14 each. Limited - bees only drew so much this season. Want two? I'll hold them.
Two is perfect. Thank you, she'll love it.
You at Briarwood this Saturday? Can you hold a case of pints for me at the booth? Paying cash.
Booth's up Saturday, usual spot. Case of 12 pints is $120 cash. I'll set it under the table with your name. Come before noon, things move fast.
Got there at 10. Thanks, all good.
Is the raw honey unfiltered and unpasteurized? Looking for local raw honey for allergies. Want to be sure before buying.
Raw, unfiltered, never pasteurized. Strained through mesh to pull wax bits, that's it. Pollen stays in. Local hives, all Philadelphia area forage.
Want to order honey as a gift for my dad. Can someone pick it up under my name, or do I need to be there? One quart in a nice jar if possible.
Anyone can grab it, just give me the name. Got a hex jar that looks nice for gifts, quart is $20. I'll tie a bit of twine on it. Ready by Friday.
He picked it up Saturday. Loved the jar. Thanks.
Spring batch isn't out yet I know. Can I pre-order 3 pints now and pick up once next harvest is jarred? Happy to pay ahead.
Is your honey local to Philadelphia? Where are the hives? I buy honey produced close to home for the pollen.
All local. 12 hives, all within the Philadelphia area. Bees forage neighborhood gardens and tree line. Can't get more local than that.
Do you sell bulk? Go through a lot of honey baking. Price on a half gallon or gallon?
Half gallon $60, gallon $115. Bring your own container or I jar it, your call. Gallon's heavy, carry it flat. Let me know when you want it.
My honey crystallized in the jar. Still good? How do I fix it? Wasn't sure if it went bad.
Crystallizing is normal, means it's raw. Not spoiled. Set jar in warm water, not boiling, till it clears. Don't microwave it, kills the good stuff.
Cedarbrook receiving. Need to restock the front display. Two cases of 1 lb jars on your next run. Send wholesale invoice. Total's over $200, flag if you need a PO.
Two cases is 24 jars, wholesale $9 a jar, $216 total. Over $200 so send a PO and I'll bill against it. Can drop them Thursday on my way through.
Is the honey safe with a tree nut allergy? No nuts in processing, right? Just being careful.
No nuts anywhere near it. Only honey and beeswax in the operation. Jarred in a clean kitchen, no nut products on site. Should be fine, but check with your doctor.
Co-op grocery desk. Last order's 1 lb jars sold fast. Can we go to two cases next restock? Send the case price so I can update the order.
Two cases, 24 jars, $9 wholesale each, $216. Over the $200 mark so I'll need a PO again. Glad they're moving. Can have them in by Friday.
Briarwood vendor coordinator. Confirming your booth for winter Saturdays. Bringing honey through the cold months or pausing until next harvest? Updating the vendor map.
Booth stays up through winter. Still got fall stock and creamed honey to move. Same corner spot. Mark me down for every Saturday till the lot closes.
Is the honey ever heated? Read raw honey shouldn't go above a certain temp. Want to confirm yours stays raw.
Never heated. Pulled from the comb cold, strained, jarred. Stays raw start to finish. That's the whole point.
